params.depth: Stamp depth (passed through to Postage Stamp).
params.hotKeyOwner: On-chain `_owner` set on the Postage Stamp contract.
params.immutable_: Whether the batch is immutable (passed through).
params.wallet: Logical "user" address; how the UI lists this batch.

params.postageStamp: Upstream Swarm Postage Stamp contract address.

createSelfCustodyBatch(address,address,u.details: Trust model      - `msg.sender` is whoever pays the BZZ (a user EOA, or an        aggregator's multicaller after a cross-chain swap fills BZZ to it).      - `wallet` is whatever address the UI wants this batch listed under.        It is unauthenticated by design — anyone can call this with any        `wallet` value. The worst a third party can do by spamming        someone else's `wallet` is gift them a batch they don't actually        own (the on-chain owner is still the spammer's `hotKeyOwner`).        UIs SHOULD show enumeration entries that don't match a known        local hot key as "external" or hide them.      - `hotKeyOwner` is the actual non-custodial owner. This contract        never receives or stores its private key.

params.nonce: Salt used to derive `batchId` (must be unique                              per registry; collision = revert from upstream).
params.wallet: The UI-side "user" address. May equal `msg.sender`                              in direct calls, or differ in aggregator flows.

increaseBatchDepth(bytes32,uint8).details: `increaseDepth` on Postage Stamp does not require additional BZZ      payment; it only changes the bucketing semantics of future      stamps. Authorisation by the original payer wallet is the natural      policy here.

predictBatchId(bytes32).details: `keccak256(abi.encode(address(this), nonce))` mirrors the Postage      Stamp contract's own `batchId` derivation when `msg.sender` is      this registry.

topUpBatch(bytes32,uint256).details: Depth is read live from upstream Postage Stamp rather than a      mirrored field, so this works correctly across `increaseDepth`      operations performed in-between.

params.batchId: Batch to top up. Must have been created via this                         registry (i.e. have an attribution entry).

_walletBatches.details: wallet → batch IDs the wallet is recorded as having paid for.

BatchCreated(bytes32,address,address,uin.notice: Emitted when a self-custody batch is created via this registry.

BatchDepthIncreased(bytes32,address,uint.notice: Emitted when a batch's depth is increased via this registry.         Gated to the original `wallet` (see {increaseBatchDepth}).

BatchToppedUp(bytes32,address,address,ui.notice: Emitted when a batch is topped up via this registry. Permissionless:         anyone can top up any batch known to this registry.

BZZ().notice: BZZ token used to pay for stamps. Set at construction; cannot         change for the lifetime of this registry.

POSTAGE_STAMP().notice: Upstream Swarm Postage Stamp contract on Gnosis Chain.

batchAttribution(bytes32).notice: batchId → attribution metadata. Empty for batches not created         through this registry (e.g. created directly on Postage Stamp         by an earlier client version).

createSelfCustodyBatch(address,address,u.notice: Create a self-custody postage batch.         BZZ flow:  msg.sender → this contract → Postage Stamp.         The caller MUST have approved this contract for at least         `initialBalancePerChunk * 2**depth` BZZ before calling.         The on-chain `_owner` set on Postage Stamp is `hotKeyOwner`,         NOT `msg.sender`. Only the holder of `hotKeyOwner`'s private         key can sign valid postage stamps for this batch.

getWalletBatchAt(address,uint256).notice: Read a single batch ID from `wallet`'s list by index.         Reverts if `index` is out of range.

getWalletBatchIds(address).notice: All batch IDs recorded under `wallet`. Newest entries are at the         end of the array (push order). Use {getWalletBatchCount} for         pagination.

increaseBatchDepth(bytes32,uint8).notice: Increase depth on an existing batch. Gated to the wallet that         originally created the batch via this registry — random callers         cannot force a depth bump.

predictBatchId(bytes32).notice: Compute the batch ID a future call to {createSelfCustodyBatch}         with the given `nonce` would produce. Useful to the UI to         pre-compute the batch ID for receipts and downstream calls         (top-up, attribution checks) before the on-chain tx lands.

topUpBatch(bytes32,uint256).notice: Top up an existing batch known to this registry. Permissionless:         anyone may top up on the original wallet's behalf.
